overview: OpenStack Keystone could allow unintended access to network services.
description:
Kay discovered that OpenStack Keystone incorrectly handled the ec2tokens
and s3tokens APIs. A remote attacker could possibly use this issue to
obtain unauthorized access and escalate privileges.

 

 

 

Kay discovered that OpenStack Keystone incorrectly handled the ec2tokens
and s3tokens APIs. A remote attacker could possibly use this issue to
obtain unauthorized access and escalate privileges.
full_text:
Ubuntu Security Notices

USN-7857-1

 

 

 

 

 

 
USN-7857-1: OpenStack Keystone vulnerability

 

 

 

 
Publication date

 

 

 
4 November 2025

 

 

 

 

 
Overview

 

 

 
OpenStack Keystone could allow unintended access to network services.

 

 

 

 

 
Releases

 

 

 

 25.10

 25.04

 24.04 LTS

 

 

 

 

 

 

 Open side navigation
 

 

 
Packages

 

 keystone - OpenStack identity service
 

 

 
Details

 

 

Kay discovered that OpenStack Keystone incorrectly handled the ec2tokens
and s3tokens APIs. A remote attacker could possibly use this issue to
obtain unauthorized access and escalate privileges.

 

 

 

Kay discovered that OpenStack Keystone incorrectly handled the ec2tokens
and s3tokens APIs. A remote attacker could possibly use this issue to
obtain unauthorized access and escalate privileges.

 

 

 

 
Update instructions

 

 

 

 

In general, a standard system update will make all the necessary changes.

 Learn more about how to get the fixes.
 

 

 

 

 
The problem can be corrected by updating your system to the following package versions:

 

 

 

 

 Ubuntu Release
 Package Version
 

 25.10

questing

 keystone – 

2:28.0.0-0ubuntu1.1

 python3-keystone – 

2:28.0.0-0ubuntu1.1

 

 25.04

plucky

 keystone – 

2:27.0.0-0ubuntu1.1

 python3-keystone – 

2:27.0.0-0ubuntu1.1

 

 24.04

LTS noble

 keystone – 

2:25.0.0-0ubuntu1.1
